ref: The people found out he was there, and they sent word to everyone who lived in this part of the country. So they brought all the sick people to Jesus.
text: A tẽe 'lɔ dẽme na wa wõ a wʊ yele ba 'yɛr lʊɔr a paal 'lɔ wʊ jaa na ba waan bala jaa an ɔɔrɔ,
translated: The man went to the farm and worked hard until he was tired and then he went home to eat.

ref: They left in a boat for a place where they could be alone.
text: Ba kpɛ naa a gboro bala tɛɛ chen a jie 'lɔ̃nɔ̃.
translated: He went to the market and bought many things.

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: Ka wʊ 'yɛr kʊ ba, ‘Yɩ mɩ chen a n puo pɔ tɩ ɩn yab yɩn naa a lɛ jaa na sɛɛn.’
translated: If you want to go, 'Yɩ mɩ chen a n puo pɔ tɩ ɩn yab yɩn naa a lɛ jaa na sɛɛn.

ref: “Wonderful!” his master replied. “You are a good and faithful servant. I left you in charge of only a little, but now I will put you in charge of much more. Come and share in my happiness!”
text: “Ka a wʊ nɩkpɛ̃ɛ 'yɛr kʊ wʊ, ‘Fʊ tʊ̃n vɩla, gbãgbamãɛ lɛ fʊ ɩ. Fʊ tara na yelmãɛ a bʊ̃bile pɔ, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔ fʊn ka fʊ kaara bʊ̃yɔɔ. Wa pʊɔ ma ka sɩ 'mataa dire a nʊ̃ɔ.’
translated: If you are going to the market, go and say, "I want to buy some, give me a good price.  I will give you money, I will give you a good price.  Do not cheat me.

ref: he got into the boat with the disciples and crossed to the territory near Dalmanutha. A Sign from Heaven (Matthew
text: Ajienaa, 'lɔ nɩ a wʊ poturbo 'mantaa kpɛ a gboro pɔ, na ba chen a Dalmanuta paal pɔ.
translated: If you want to go to the market, go early in the morning, before the sun rises, so that you can buy goods from Dalmanuta.

ref: The angel said, “Get up and take the child and his mother back to Israel. The people who wanted to kill him are now dead.”
text: “Vaa ir 'mɔɔ a bie nɩ a wʊ ma na fʊ chiin a Isɩral wɛr pɔ, a bala na dɔ̃ɔ bʊɔrɔ a bie kʊfʊ kpi naa.”
translated: If you go to Israel, you will see many beautiful things there, and you will be amazed by the things you see.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ na sʊɔr na nyɛ naa, ka a 'lɔ mɩ na yɔbɔ nyɛ, ka a pãn mɩ yuo kʊ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na kpoor.
translated: If you see a snake and a bird, take the snake and give it to the person who saw the snake first.

ref: Many prophets and good people were eager to see what you see and to hear what you hear. But I tell you they did not see or hear. Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Mark
text: Yelmãɛ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ kʊrɔ yɩ ɩka Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛ'yɛrbɛ yɔɔ nɩ nɩtorsɩ yɔɔ ta bʊɔrɔ na ɩka ba nyɛ bon 'lɔ yɩn nyɛ tɩchɛ baa nyɛ ɛ, na ba ta wõ bon 'lɔ yɩn wõ tɩchɛ baa tʊ̃ɔ wõ ɛ.
translated: If you want to go to Nãaŋmɩn, you have to go through the forest, and if you want to go through the forest, you have to be careful, because there are many dangers in the forest.

ref: Everyone who has something will be given more, and they will have more than enough. But everything will be taken from those who don't have anything.
text: Nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na tara yɔɔ, ban kʊ wʊn yɔɔ pʊɔ ka ba tara yɔɔ tɩchɛ a nɩrɛ 'lɔ na ba tara bon jaa ɛ, a bãlãa 'lɔ wʊn tara gba ban faan yin a wʊ sɛ̃.
translated: If you go to the river, you will see many fish. If you go to the river to catch fish, you will see many fish there, and you will catch many fish. You will be happy.

ref: So the chief priests met with the leaders and decided to bribe the soldiers with a lot of money.
text: A bɔɔrlo nãa nɩ a wʊ nɩbɛrɛ na wa lɔ̃ɔ taa, ba guor taa de libiyɔɔ kʊ a sojasɩ
translated: The man went to the market to buy some things, he bought some meat and gave it to the soldiers.

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: a Nãa Solomon gba nɩ a wʊ tɩɩr wʊ jaa ba dɔ̃ɔ su fuu ka wʊ vɩɛlɛ gar a mɔpuul a nyãna ɛ.
translated: Solomon Nãa went to the market to buy some food and he met his friend there.

ref: His threshing fork is in his hand, and he is ready to separate the wheat from the husks. He will store the wheat in a barn and burn the husks in a fire that never goes out. The Baptism of Jesus (Mark
text: A ŋmayɩɛlaa ben a wʊ nũu pɔ, ka wʊ na yɩɛl a mʊ̃ɩ a vafʊ jie na wʊ kpaar a bie 'yɔ̃ɔ a boor pɔ tɩ chʊm a sɔ̃sɔɔr nɩ bũu 'lɔ na ba tara kpĩinfʊ bar.”
translated: If you want to eat, you should go and find something to eat, because if you don't find something to eat, you will become very hungry and you will be unable to work.

ref: So Peter said to him, “Lord, it is good for us to be here! Let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
text: Pita 'yɛr kʊ Yesu, “N Soro, a vɩla na ka sɩn kpɩɛrɛ a ka. Ala ɩ fʊ sɔɔ naa, ɩn 'yɔ̃ɔn sɛɛr ata a ka na, ka fʊ so a ãsʊɔ, ka Mosesɩ mɩ so a ãsʊɔ, ka Elaija mɩ so a ãsʊɔ.”
translated: Pita said to Yesu, “Lord, we believe that you are the Christ, the Son of the living God. We believe that you are the one who was prophesied about, the one who will come to save us, just as Moses saved us, and Elijah saved us.

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “Sɩrɛ jaa lɛ a Isaiya dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr a yɩn nɛɛr ayi dẽme 'yɛr na wʊ sɛb. Nãaŋmɩn 'yɛr naa, ‘A yɩn nɩbɛ banyãna 'yɔ̃ɔnɔ̃ ma naa nɩ a yɩ nɛɛ, tɩchɛ ka a yɩ pʊtɩɛr jãa a n sɛ̃.
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “Have you not read what Isaiah the prophet said: ‘The Lord said to my servant, “You shall not be afraid, for I am with you; I will strengthen you and help you; I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.”

ref: Then Jesus told the man, “Hold out your hand.” The man did, and it became as healthy as the other one.
text: Wʊ 'yɛr kʊ a daba 'lɔ nũu na kpi, “Tur a fʊ nũu.” A wʊ tur ka a nũu wʊ jaa sa kaara a nũu nyã sʊɔ kaar.
translated: He said to the woman, "Come, let us go." She said, "Let us go to the place where we will meet our people."
